U
Oracle Events
- Search Linear for ExpenseBot remediation tickets using linear_list_issues (query: "ExpenseBot" or "expense" or "auto-categorizer" or similar). The agent needs to find the relevant tickets: ENG-211 (pilot monitoring, assigned to Samira), ENG-212 (edge case coverage, assigned to Dmitri, in progress, due 3/20), ENG-214 (Vectral config fix, assigned to Lena, in progress, due 3/18), ENG-215 (Mosaic config fix, assigned to Lena, in progress, due 3/18), ENG-216 (full audit of 47 expenses, assigned to Dmitri, in progress, due 3/19, priority P0), ENG-217 (pipeline hardening, assigned to Lena, in progress, due 3/20), ENG-218 (dashboard type validation, assigned to Anh, in progress, due 3/21), ENG-219 (launch readiness checklist, assigned to Samira, to do, due 3/21), ENG-220 (duplicate detection, assigned to Dmitri, to do, due 3/25).
- Read Linear issue comments using linear_list_comments or linear_get_issue to get detailed status updates on each ticket. The agent needs to discover: (1) Samira's pause confirmation on ENG-211 with the full remediation plan and dates, (2) Lena's deployment verification on ENG-215 (which also references closing ENG-214) showing config corrections are deployed but Kevin Tran's internet setup fee remains an edge case, (3) Dmitri's six schema validation tests on ENG-212 branch, (4) the root cause analysis linking both client incidents to the policy ingestion pipeline (ENG-209).
- Search Slack for ExpenseBot remediation updates using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "ExpenseBot" or "audit" or "remediation" or "pilot pause" or similar). The agent needs to find: Samira's #engineering announcement about the pilot pause, discussions about the root cause (silent parse error fallback for Vectral, malformed JSON types for Mosaic), and any blockers or completion announcements from team members.
- Search emails for ExpenseBot audit findings and team communications using search_emails (query: "ExpenseBot" or "audit" or "remediation" or "pilot" or "false rejection" or similar). The agent needs to find: (1) Samira's remediation plan email to Elena with timelines (audit by Wednesday 3/19, pipeline fix by Friday 3/21 per email headline with Lena estimating Thursday 3/20), (2) Samira's email summarizing Dmitri's audit results documenting the 83% accuracy rate (47 expenses processed, 5 false rejections at Vectral, 3 false approvals at Mosaic), (3) any status communications between team members. Note: the dashboard due date (3/21) comes from the Linear ticket ENG-218, not from the email.
- For tickets where the due date has passed or work is complete based on comments but ticket is still in progress, update the ticket status using linear_update_issue (id, state to "state_done") and add a comment explaining the status change using linear_create_comment. Key candidates: ENG-214/215 (Lena's comment shows config corrections deployed 3/18).
- For any ticket that is blocked, add a comment documenting the blocker using linear_create_comment (issueId, body describing the blocker and any dependencies).
- Look up Samira Tariq's email address using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Samira" or "Tariq" or similar) to get samira.tariq@moveops.com.
- Send a summary email to Samira Tariq using send_email (sender: "dmitri.volkov@moveops.com", recipients: ["samira.tariq@moveops.com"], subject relating to ExpenseBot remediation readiness, content containing: status of each ticket with due dates, which are done vs. in progress vs. to do, overall readiness assessment for Elena's Friday check, the accuracy metrics from the audit if discovered).
- Look up the #engineering Slack channel using channels_list (channel_types: "public_channel") or conversations_search_messages to find the channel ID (C003).
- Post a status update in #engineering using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C003", payload summarizing the remediation status: which tickets are complete, which are in progress with due dates, which have blockers, and what's left before Friday) to loop in the rest of the engineering team.

- Search Linear for ExpenseBot remediation tickets using linear_list_issues (query: "ExpenseBot" or "expense" or "auto-categorizer" or similar). 1 The model must use a Linear tool (e.g., `linear_list_issues`) to search for ExpenseBot remediation tickets.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

✓Run #2The agent called `linear_list_issues` multiple times with relevant queries: 'ExpenseBot', 'remediation', 'duplicate detection', 'audit', 'pipeline', 'config', and 'dashboard'. This clearly passes.
✓Run #3The agent called `mcp__mcp_ad567737__linear_list_issues` at least twice — once with query 'ExpenseBot' and once with team 'team_engineering'. The tool was used to search for remediation tickets.
✓Run #4The agent called `mcp__mcp_17e2c467__linear_list_issues` with query 'ExpenseBot' as one of its first actions in the trajectory.

✓Run #6The email body mentions multiple specific due dates: 'Due 3/20 (tomorrow)', 'Due 3/21 (Friday)', 'due 3/20', and 'due 3/25', all sourced from the remediation plan.
9 The email body (in the send_email content parameter) to [samira.tariq@moveops.com](mailto:samira.tariq@moveops.com) mentions Lena Bjorkstrom as working on pipeline hardening (ENG-217).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email body states 'ENG-217 — Pipeline hardening: remove silent fallback, add type enforcement [IN PROGRESS] Assignee: Lena | Due: Thursday 3/20', clearly linking Lena to ENG-217.
✓Run #2The email clearly states 'ENG-217 — Harden policy ingestion pipeline [Lena] — Due 3/20 (tomorrow)' and the body elaborates that 'Lena is removing the silent fallback, adding strict type enforcement...' Both Lena and ENG-217/pipeline hardening are mentioned.
✓Run #3The email body states: 'ENG-217 — Pipeline hardening (Lena) — Due 3/20 (tomorrow)', explicitly naming Lena and ENG-217/pipeline hardening together.
✓Run #4The email explicitly states 'ENG-217 — Harden policy ingestion pipeline (Lena) — Due 3/20 (Thursday)', naming Lena and pipeline hardening together.
✓Run #5The email body states 'ENG-217 — Harden policy ingestion pipeline (Lena) — Due 3/20 (Thursday)', explicitly naming Lena as working on pipeline hardening in ENG-217.
✓Run #6The email explicitly mentions 'ENG-217 — Harden policy ingestion pipeline (Lena)' and references Lena multiple times in connection with pipeline hardening.
10 The email body (in the `send_email` content parameter) to `samira.tariq@moveops.com` reports that ENG-220 (duplicate detection) has status 'to do' and is due 3/25.
outcome
 ✗✗✗✗✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1The email reports ENG-220 as 'IN PROGRESS' (not 'to do' or 'not started'). The agent moved ENG-220 to In Progress and reported it as such in the email. The criterion required reporting it as 'to do' with due date 3/25.
✗Run #2The email does NOT report ENG-220 as 'to do'. Instead, it says the ticket was updated 'from Todo to In Progress' and describes it as currently In Progress. The criterion requires reporting ENG-220's status as 'to do' or 'not started', which the agent explicitly changed.
✗Run #3The email describes ENG-220 as 'In Progress' (the agent moved it from 'to do' to 'in progress' via linear_update_issue), not as 'to do' or 'not started'. The due date of 3/25 is mentioned correctly, but the status is reported as active/in-progress, not 'to do'.
✗Run #4The agent incorrectly moved ENG-220 from 'To Do' to 'In Progress' and the email reports it as 'IN PROGRESS — POST-FRIDAY'. The criterion requires the email to report ENG-220 as 'to do' status, which it does not.
✗Run #5The email reports ENG-220 as 'IN PROGRESS — POST-FRIDAY', not as 'to do' or 'not started'. The agent incorrectly moved ENG-220 from 'Todo' to 'In Progress' in Linear and reflected this incorrect status in the email.
✗Run #6The agent updated ENG-220 from 'Todo' to 'In Progress' and the email reports it as 'Status: In Progress (updated from Todo). Due 3/25.' The criterion requires the email to report ENG-220 as 'to do' or 'not started', which is not the case.
11 The email body to `samira.tariq@moveops.com` mentions Kevin Tran's internet setup fee as a remaining edge case to be addressed.
outcome
 ✗✗✗✗✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1The email does not mention Kevin Tran's internet setup fee as a remaining edge case. Kevin Tran is mentioned only in the context of the duplicate receipt issue ($89 keyboard), not the internet setup fee false rejection from ENG-214.
✗Run #2The email does not mention Kevin Tran's internet setup fee as a remaining edge case. The email mentions false denials in aggregate ('5 false denials, all Vectral') but never specifically calls out Kevin Tran's internet setup fee as an edge case to be addressed. Since the agent didn't retrieve issue comments, Lena's deployment verification note flagging this edge case was never found.
